text:
**Key constant** (line 18):
```rust
pub(crate) const FABRIC_INJECTION_MARKER: &str = "[tenex-edge]";
```

The marker is used to prevent echo loops—the daemon checks if `prompt.trim_start().starts_with(FABRIC_INJECTION_MARKER)` (line 24) to determine if text is already-published and should not be re-published. [...] Today, **the envelope text itself is the echo-suppression mechanism.** Every injected envelope starts with the literal marker `[tenex-edge]` (`injection.rs:18`). When the pty path pastes a mention into the agent's pane, the harness fires `user-prompt-submit`, and `chat_publish.rs:172` checks `is_fabric_injection(prompt)` — *"does this prompt start with `[tenex-edge]`?"* — to decide **not** to re-publish it. Without that, the human's message would get re-published into the channel as if the agent said it (twice, on publish-retry).

So your desired bare format (`@developer hey there`, no marker) **breaks echo suppression.** You can't have bare injection *and* keep the textual-marker mechanism. This isn't a blocker — it's actually a chance to fix a fragile design (the marker also eats any human who legitimately types `[tenex-edge]…`). The fix: **move echo-suppression off the visible text and onto daemon-side bookkeeping.** When the pty path pastes, stash `hash(pasted_text)` (or the delivered `event_id`s) in a short-TTL per-session ring in `DaemonState`; `rpc_user_prompt` checks that ring instead of a text prefix. Then the envelope can be *any* shape, including bare.

I'll assume we make that change in all proposals below. [...] 1. **Retire the `[tenex-edge]` text marker for echo** (`injection.rs:18`, `chat_publish.rs:172`). Replace with a per-session `recently_injected` ring in `DaemonState` (hash of pasted text + TTL ~60s), recorded in `inject_pending_messages_pub` after paste, checked in `rpc_user_prompt`. This is what unblocks bare/minimal injection — and it's more robust than the prefix it replaces. [...] User: 1. yes, retire the marker check -- that's weak [...] - **`src/daemon/server/echo_guard.rs`** *(new)* — per-session hash ring (60s TTL) replacing the `[tenex-edge]` text marker. Records what the paste path typed; `rpc_user_prompt` consumes the match.
- **`src/injection.rs`** — rewritten into `render_pty_mention` (forms ①/②, sender-classified via whitelist), `render_hook_mention` (③, wrapped + CLI hint), `render_ambient` (④, wrapped timeline). Message-id dropped everywhere. Speaker `<@name>` prefixes resolve via the profile cache; relative-time shown per policy (never for live pty turns, always for ambient, only-when-stale for hook mentions).
